taskDescription: Complete the crossword on the Back Region anatomy

words.clue: Venous plexus in the epidural space that provides a valveless route for metastatic spread
Critical radicular artery arising from T8-L2, usually left-sided, supplying the thoracolumbar cord
Terminal end of the spinal cord, located at vertebral level L1-L2 in adults
Bundle of nerve roots below L2 in the lumbar cistern
Strand of pia mater extending from the conus medullaris to the coccyx
Triangle in the suboccipital region containing the vertebral artery and C1 nerve
Space between the dura and the periosteum of the vertebral canal
Meningeal layer that is the outermost and thickest, forming the dural sac to S2
Preferred vertebral interspace for lumbar puncture in adults
Single artery supplying the anterior two-thirds of the spinal cord

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
